name: 了 — Completed Action & Change of State

cards.grammarNote: 了 after a verb marks completion. Place it directly after the verb to show the action finished.

cards.romanized: Wǒ mǎi le yī běn shū.

cards.front: She drank a glass of water.
cards.romanized: Tā hē le yī bēi shuǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 了 at the end of a sentence signals a new state or change. Something has shifted — it wasn't raining before, but it is now.

cards.front: I understand now. / I get it.
I didn't eat. / I haven't eaten.
cards.grammarNote: Negate completed actions with 没 (méi) or 没有 (méiyǒu) before the verb. Never use 不 with 了 for completed actions. Drop 了 in the negative.

cards.front: The store is closed now. (it just closed)
